Patch Set 9:

Anyone one else want to give this a look over.

Patch Set 9:

Yes, I do, but cannot ATM.

Patch Set 9:

Wouldn't it make sense to add specific trace events to the Lustre code instead of migrating lustre debugging subsystem to ftrace buffers? Writing strings to the ftrace buffer, even with separate arguments containing function names, files and lines is not very consistent with the tracepoint ideology. It's closer to the dynamic debug concept.

Patch Set 9:

Andrew I had a discussion with Oleg about your comment. Yes you are correct each tracepoint has to be unique. I updated the patch locally for all the traces that don't print to the console. If the tracepoint need to print to the console then its not so easy to be unique. See for example usb xhci driver. How it is handled. So I'm thinking on to deal with two things. One is the calls to trace_set_clr_event() has gotten very long. Need to find a way to list the trace in module to make the filtering cleaner. Next is resolving a way to make trace printing to console unique. Soon a patch will be coming.

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 18:

So before we could write

CERROR("insert_proc: error %d\n", rc);

in the current function, just after the failure. But now we have to write

TRACE_EVENT_CONDITION(libcfs_error_startup_procfs,
        TP_PROTO(struct ratelimit_state *rs, const char *msg_file,
                 int msg_line, const char *msg_fn, int rc),
        TP_ARGS(rs, msg_file, msg_line, msg_fn, rc),
        TP_CONDITION(__ratelimit(rs)),
        TP_STRUCT__entry(
                __string(msg_file, msg_file ? msg_file : "<nofile>")
                __field(int, msg_line)
                __string(msg_func, msg_fn ? msg_fn : "<nofunc>")
                __field(int, rc)
        ),
        TP_fast_assign(
                __assign_str(msg_file, kbasename(msg_file));
                __entry->msg_line = msg_line;
                __assign_str(msg_func, msg_fn);
                __entry->rc = rc;
        ),
        TP_printk("(%s:%d:%s) insert_proc: error %d",
                  __get_str(msg_file), __entry->msg_line, __get_str(msg_func),
                  __entry->rc)
);

#define trace_cerror_startup_procfs(rc)                                 \
do {                                                                    \
        trace_libcfs_error_startup_procfs(&libcfs_trace_rs, __FILE__,   \
                                          __LINE__, __func__, rc);      \
                                                                        \
        CERROR("insert_proc: error %d\n", rc);                          \
} while (0)

in a different file and the put

trace_cerror_startup_procfs(rc)

in the original function. Usually stuff like this is bad for the writer of the code but good for the reader. Or the other way around. But this is bad for the writer and for the reader.

I think tracepoints should be introduced to lustre. I do not think they are a good way to replace all CDEBUGs. Has the kernel replaced all printks with tracepoints? No.

Patch Set 18:

Ah I see what you are saying. I had this discussion some time ago with Andreas but what debug message is really important. Currently Lustre is way to liberal with its sprinkle of debugging messages. With the move to tracepoint, which trades easy to follow with advance filtering, will make people think twice about sprinkling debug statements in the code.

We have to consider what is the goal with having a debug statement. In many cases with script foo, perf and dynamic tracing you can extract complex data structure to debug something. We don't need to always record that info on each run. Can you join the LWG meeting (https://meet.intel.com/joseph.gmitter/F6BQ4FST) today at  11AM Pacific/ 2PM Eastern. We can discuss this.

Patch Set 18:

Yes, I'll join the call.
Abandoned

parents.subject: LU-9597 ofd: fix race for project setattr

commit.subject: LU-8980 libcfs: add tracepoint support for libcfs
commit.message:
LU-8980 libcfs: add tracepoint support for libcfs

Add initial trace events to the libcfs module. With
this we can now collect data using the perf utility.
Each traditonal lustre debug macro is turned into
an unique tracepoint event. Any debugging macros
moved over to trace point will now handling printing
message to the console.

Change-Id: I0fc6efc24fd9495fae2829990f74b1a72491baf8
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
